text.title: nosql-mongodb-patterns Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for nosql-mongodb-patterns as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Unbounded embedded arrays — appending events, log entries, or tags to the same document indefinitely.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: MongoDB documents have a 16 MB size cap. Once hit, writes fail. Queries that load the full document become progressively slower. Use referencing, the bucket pattern, or capped collections instead. Document the array cap in the schema file.

failure-mode.description: Over-embedding: duplicating deeply nested sub-documents that are updated independently.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: When an embedded sub-document changes frequently and is referenced from many parent documents, every update requires touching multiple parents. This creates write amplification and consistency risks. Reference instead.

failure-mode.description: Missing indexes on query fields — especially on high-cardinality fields used in filters on large collections.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: A COLLSCAN on a 10M-document collection can take seconds. MongoDB's explain()'s IXSCAN vs COLLSCAN is the definitive test; run it before every production query.
